steps.title: Look for a plea to Virtue
steps.content: The claim requires two things: Wheatley must address Virtue directly, and she must ask Virtue to help her reach a future goal. A quotation that merely discusses Virtue, tries to understand it, or says Virtue is nearby would not fully illustrate the claim.

steps.title: Separate plea from description
steps.content: Choice B emphasizes how hard Virtue is to comprehend. Choice C describes an attempt to understand Virtue despite wisdom being out of reach. Choice D comforts the speaker by saying Virtue is nearby, but it is not a request for help. The matching quotation asks Virtue to attend and guide the speaker toward endless life and bliss. The answer is A.
